title: Long-Term Effects of Environmental Lead on Erythropoietin Production in Young Adults: A Follow-Up Study of a Prospective Cohort in Kosovo.
ehr_evidence: The study uses a prospective birth cohort and a biorepository, not EHR.
summary: A follow-up study of a prospective birth cohort in Kosovo to evaluate the long-term effects of prenatal and early childhood lead (Pb) exposure on erythropoietin (EPO) production in young adults.
key_findings: High environmental Pb exposure required increased EPO production to maintain normal Hgb at ages 4.5 and 6.5.
By ages 9.5 and 12, participants were unable to hyper-produce EPO to maintain normal Hgb, suggesting cumulative renal toxicity.
Reduction in Pb exposure may reverse the impact on EPO production.
population: 101 participants (mean age 24.9 years) from a prospective birth cohort in Kosovo
limitations: Recruitment challenges in Prishtina due to city expansion and demographic changes
data_availability_statement: All pertinent data are in our study files and can be made available upon request.
